# Lighting System

> Directional, point, and spot light rendering

**Category:** Integration · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** integration · godot

## What this does

Directional, point, and spot light rendering

Specifies 5 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## What must be true

- **implementation:** Feature implemented in C++ engine core
- **platform_agnostic:** Works across desktop, web, mobile platforms

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Directional Lights** — when Sun-like lights from infinity is requested, then Sun-like lights from infinity completed.
- **Point Lights** — when Omni-directional radial lights is requested, then Omni-directional radial lights completed.
- **Spot Lights** — when Cone-shaped lights with falloff is requested, then Cone-shaped lights with falloff completed.
- **Light Intensity** — when Control brightness is requested, then Control brightness completed.
- **Shadow Mapping** — when Real-time shadow casting is requested, then Real-time shadow casting completed.
